title: Venice Art Show Jury Says: No Awards for Russia or Israel
summary:
The Venice Biennale is one of the biggest art shows in the world. Countries build their own special rooms, called pavilions, to show off their best artists. It's like a giant art competition between nations. But this year, something big happened.

The jury that decides who wins the top prizes said they will NOT give awards to Russia or Israel. Why? Because the International Criminal Court (ICC) has charged the leaders of both countries with crimes against humanity. That means the court believes they did very bad things to people. Russia's President Vladimir Putin and Israel's Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu both have arrest warrants from the ICC.

The jury is made up of five women from different countries. They said they don't want to give awards to countries whose leaders are charged with such serious crimes. Many artists agreed. Almost 10,000 people signed a letter saying Russia shouldn't be in the show at all. The European Union even threatened to take away money from the Biennale because Russia was invited. Some people think the jury should have named Israel directly. But the jury says their decision is clear enough. It's a big debate about art and politics.
why_it_matters: This story shows that art competitions can get involved in big world issues, and it asks you to think about whether artists and art shows should take sides in politics.

keywords.explanation: a big international art show held every two years in Venice, Italy
special rooms or buildings that countries use to show their art
a group of people who decide the winners
very bad acts like murder or torture committed against many people

questions.question: What event did the jury make a decision about?
Which two countries are affected by the jury's decision?
Why did the jury decide not to give awards to those countries?
options: Because their art was not good enough
Because their leaders are charged with crimes against humanity by the ICC
Because the artists refused to participate
Because the countries withdrew from the show
questions.correct_answer: Because their leaders are charged with crimes against humanity by the ICC

questions.question: Who are the leaders charged by the ICC mentioned in the article?
How many people signed a letter saying Russia shouldn't be in the show?
What did the European Union threaten to do?
options: Take away money from the Biennale
questions.correct_answer: Take away money from the Biennale

background_read: The Venice Biennale started in 1895 and is one of the oldest and most famous art shows in the world.
The International Criminal Court (ICC) was established in 2002 to prosecute individuals for serious international crimes.
Art and politics have often mixed before; for example, during World War II, some countries were not allowed to participate in the Biennale.
Article_Structure: WHO: The Venice Biennale jury (five women from different countries)
WHAT: They decided not to give awards to Russia or Israel because of ICC charges against their leaders
WHERE: Venice Biennale, an art show in Italy
WHY: The jury wants to uphold moral standards and not honor countries whose leaders face serious charges

perspectives.description: Believes it's right to stand up against serious crimes and not honor countries with such leaders.
Thinks art should stay separate from politics, and that punishing the whole country isn't fair to the artists.
Sees the decision as a strong statement, but notes that not naming Israel directly caused confusion and criticism.
